title: Nasdaq-100 Index® June 2026 Quarterly Changes
author: Nasdaq
description: Nasdaq announced the results of its June 2026 quarterly rebalance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, effective June 22, 2026. Five companies were added to the index: Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne. Five companies were removed: Charter Communications, Cognizant Technology Solutions, Insmed, Verisk Analytics, and Zscaler.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Added to the Nasdaq-100 Index, indicating recognition as one of the largest Nasdaq-listed non-financial companies

title: Will Verisk's Valuable Data Moat Hold, or Will AI Commoditize It?
author: Bryan White
description: Verisk Analytics' stock has fallen nearly 50% amid investor concerns that AI could commoditize its data assets and erode its competitive advantages in the insurance industry. However, the company's regulatory position, proprietary databases, and strong profitability (56% EBITDA margins, $1.2B free cash flow) provide defensibility. Verisk is proactively integrating AI into its platforms, though revenue growth has slowed to 3.9% in Q1 2026. The stock trades at 22x earnings, presenting a potential buying opportunity for patient investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite near-term headwinds from slowing growth and AI disruption concerns, Verisk maintains strong fundamentals with 56% EBITDA margins, $1.2B free cash flow, and a defensible regulatory moat across all 50 U.S. states. The stock's 50% decline has created an attractive valuation at 22x earnings, and management's aggressive share buybacks ($1.4B in Q1) and 11% dividend increase signal confidence. The company is proactively addressing AI threats through its own GenAI tools.

title: Insurance Analytics Market Size to Hit USD 72.39 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Insurance Analytics Market is valued at $17.60 billion in 2025 and expected to reach $72.39 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 15.19%. Growth is driven by fraud detection, AI adoption, cloud computing, and alternative data sources like telematics and IoT sensors. Software components and cloud deployment dominate the market, while SMEs and government agencies represent the fastest-growing segments.

title: The Council Announces Continued Growth of Membership & Sponsorship Communities
author: Na
description: The Council of Insurance Agents & Brokers announced the addition of three new member firms: GIS Benefits, Steadfast, and Balavant Insurance Group, bringing the total of new members in 2026 to nine. The Council also announced expanded sponsorships and increased investment commitments from multiple industry partners ahead of its Employee Benefits Leadership Forum in Colorado Springs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Elevated partnership status from Platinum to Diamond Partner, indicating increased commitment and confidence in the Council's value
Increased investment from Gold to Platinum Partner, showing expanded commitment to the association

title: Catching Fake Insurance Claims: Why Image Verification Has Become Essential
author: Na
description: Insurance fraud costs the US $308.6 billion annually, with a significant portion involving fake or recycled images submitted through digital claims. The shift to online claims processing has enabled new fraud tactics including stock photo submissions, image manipulation, and AI-generated synthetic damage photos. Reverse image search and AI-driven verification technologies are emerging as effective tools to detect fraud, with potential savings of $160 billion through 2032. Insurance companies implementing layered verification systems at intake are catching fraudulent claims before they become costly.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Verisk is cited as a data source providing statistics on suspicious claim images, but the article does not discuss their products, services, or business performance in relation to fraud detection solutions.
